entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop planted in mid-November and harvested in mid-March with a grain yield of 2-3 tons/ha

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop planted after wheat and harvested in early June, providing grain, fodder, and green manure

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal crop transplanted in July and harvested in early November with a grain yield of about 4.5 ton/ha

entities.name: TRIPLE CROPPING
entities.description: A cropping system involving the sequential planting of wheat, cowpea, and rice in a single year

entities.name: SUPPLEMENTAL IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of additional water to crops in irrigated fields

entities.name: SUDAN SAVANNA ZONE
entities.description: The specific ecological zone in Nigeria where the trials were conducted

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per hectare

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: LIVESTOCK NUTRITION
entities.description: The provision of nutrients to livestock through crop residues or fodder

entities.name: CEREAL-CEREAL ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of planting cereal crops in succession

entities.name: GREEN MANURE
entities.description: The practice of growing crops to be incorporated into the soil to improve fertility

entities.name: FODDER
entities.description: Agricultural produce used as feed for livestock
